description: You are a Database Architect for an e-commerce retailer managing the RETAIL_DW schema in Oracle (Host=localhost, Port=1521, Service=XEPDB1, User=retail_admin, Password=Retail2024). To support historical financial reporting, implement a Slowly Changing Dimension (SCD Type 2) architecture. Perform an initial load of 'SmartHome' category products from SOURCE_PRODUCTS into DIM_PRODUCTS using SEQ_PRODUCT_KEY.NEXTVAL, setting valid_from to 2020-01-01, valid_to to 9999-12-31, and is_current to 'Y'. Create a row-level trigger named TRG_PRODUCT_SCD2 on SOURCE_PRODUCTS to handle SCD Type 2 updates for unit_price and category by expiring current records and inserting new active records. Develop a point-in-time join view named HISTORICAL_SALES_REVENUE_VW joining SALES_FACT to DIM_PRODUCTS (where sale_date falls between valid_from and valid_to) to output sale_id, product_id, sale_date, quantity, historical_unit_price, and a calculated line_total. Finally, create a view named SCD2_OVERLAP_ANOMALIES_VW that identifies product_ids in DIM_PRODUCTS with overlapping validity periods.

metadata.dynamic_verification: Trigger is tested post-task by injecting an UPDATE statement.
